name: bots
context: Build Slack bots with Bolt.js: listen for events, respond to commands, handle interactions
info: This skill covers building Slack bots using the Bolt.js framework. Bolt handles the low-level plumbing — request verification, OAuth, event parsing — so you can focus on bot logic. Bots can listen for events (messages, reactions, app mentions), register slash commands, handle interactive components (buttons, menus, modals), and use Socket Mode for firewall-friendly connections without a public URL. The framework supports both HTTP and Socket Mode transports. See [[slack/events]] for deeper coverage of the Events API and [[slack/block-kit]] for interactive UI components.
triggers: create a slack app with interactions

sections.title: Bot setup
sections.body: Install `@slack/bolt`. Create an App instance with `token` (bot token), `signingSecret`, and optionally `socketMode: true` with `appToken` for Socket Mode. Call `app.start()` to launch. See [[slack#authentication]] for token setup.

sections.title: Slash commands
sections.body: Register commands in app settings. Handle with `app.command("/name", handler)`. Always `ack()` within 3 seconds. Use `respond()` for follow-up messages. Commands include `user_id`, `text`, `channel_id`, and `trigger_id` (for opening modals).

sections.title: Event listeners
sections.body: Use `app.message(pattern, handler)` for message matching, `app.event(type, handler)` for any event type. Subscribe to events in app settings. Common events: `message`, `app_mention`, `reaction_added`, `member_joined_channel`. See [[slack/events]] for full event reference.

sections.title: Interactive components
sections.body: Handle button clicks and menu selections with `app.action(action_id, handler)`. Handle modal submissions with `app.view(callback_id, handler)`. Always `ack()` interactions. Use [[slack/block-kit#actions]] for building interactive layouts and [[slack/block-kit#modals]] for modal views.

sections.title: Socket Mode
sections.body: Socket Mode uses WebSocket instead of HTTP. No public URL needed. Enable in app settings, generate an app-level token (`xapp-`) with `connections:write` scope, pass as `appToken`. Ideal for development, internal tools, and firewall-restricted environments. Also used by [[slack/events]] for real-time event delivery.

response.description: Bot response message or action result
